research.interface: Terraform Enterprise REST API v2 (/api/v2, JSON:API): team access (team-workspaces) endpoints (stable)

official_sources.title: API documentation for Terraform Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: /api/v2 base path, Authorization: Bearer token auth, application/vnd.api+json JSON:API media type, page[number]/page[size] pagination with meta.pagination (current-page, prev-page, next-page, total-pages, total-count), JSON:API errors array shape, and 404 responses masking unauthorized resources

official_sources.title: /team-workspaces API reference for Terraform Enterprise (Team Access)
official_sources.used_for: GET /team-workspaces with required filter[workspace][id] and pagination; the team-workspaces resource type, access attribute values read/plan/write/admin/custom, and team/workspace relationships; POST create body; PATCH/DELETE /team-workspaces/:id; and the 404 'Team access not found or user unauthorized to perform action' masking semantics

research.language_rationale: There is no official HashiCorp Ruby SDK for the TFE API; the documented integration surface is the raw JSON:API REST contract at /api/v2, and Ruby's stdlib net/http plus json (with minitest for acceptance) is the idiomatic zero-dependency way to implement a small reconciler against it.

verified_facts: Team access records are listed with GET /api/v2/team-workspaces and the required filter[workspace][id] query parameter naming the workspace; the list supports page[number]/page[size] pagination with the standard meta.pagination object, and clients follow next-page until it is null.

A team access record has JSON:API type team-workspaces, an access attribute whose valid values are read, plan, write, admin, and custom, and relationships to the team (type teams) and workspace (type workspaces).

Team access is granted with POST /api/v2/team-workspaces whose body carries data.type team-workspaces, attributes.access, and the team/workspace relationship linkages; it is modified with PATCH /api/v2/team-workspaces/:id (the id repeated in data.id) and revoked with DELETE /api/v2/team-workspaces/:id.
Custom access exposes finer-grained permission attributes (runs, variables, state-versions, sentinel-mocks, workspace-locking, run-tasks) that are only editable when access is custom.

Terraform Enterprise deliberately answers 404 both for team access records that do not exist and for ones the token is not authorized to see ('Team access not found or user unauthorized to perform action'), so clients cannot distinguish deletion from lost visibility and must handle both as one case.
All requests carry Authorization: Bearer <token>, bodies use application/vnd.api+json, and errors arrive as JSON:API error documents ({"errors": [{status, title, detail}]}).
